changelog: Fixed: Browser extension overlay button downloads now work correctly for Facebook and other sites
Fixed: Long video titles no longer cause download failures — automatically truncated to safe length
Fixed: Extension folder can now be recovered automatically if accidentally deleted
Improved: Extension updates automatically on app restart (when browsers are closed)
Improved: Download commands are now cleaner and more reliable
Fixed: downloading some files failed with "Access denied" — now handled automatically
Fixed: yt-dlp showed "already up to date" even when it wasn't — version check now works correctly
Fixed: updating the app on C: drive installs silently failed or required admin but never asked — now prompts correctly when needed, and skips the prompt when not needed
Fixed: the app update installer used to show the full setup wizard — now it installs quietly in the background
Fixed: the app could close before you confirmed the admin prompt, cancelling the update — now it waits
Fixed: clicking the video download button on a webpage showed formats for a split second, then immediately showed an error
Fixed: the "use browser cookies" checkbox in the video downloader was on by default, which triggered bot detection on some sites — now off by default
Fixed: on fresh install, Aither re-downloaded yt-dlp and ffmpeg even though they were already included — now copies them from the installer instead
Fixed: ffmpeg failed to download when an update was available due to a network decompression error — now downloads correctly
Added: if a server blocks a download, Aither automatically retries with a different browser identity — up to 3 attempts before giving up
Improved: speed limit setting now saves and loads correctly across sessions
Improved: download engine is significantly more stable — fewer random failures or stuck downloads
